strengths.text: Strong senior full-stack profile with 12+ years of production experience across React, TypeScript, Next.js, backend APIs, cloud, and Web3 security systems.
Direct Kraken-relevant blockchain and client-funds-adjacent evidence from OpenZeppelin: Defender Secure Audit, OSS Relayer, RPC retry/failover, cloud KMS signers, and a critical nonce race fix.

Payment and transaction integration evidence exists through Stripe billing, Transbank payment flows, and transaction-related Express.js API work; user clarified those payment integrations included both user-facing frontend work and backend/API work.
Concrete mobile and real-time evidence is now available through React Native apps delivered at Resit and WebSockets-based courier coordinate transmission at Dxpress.
Frontend quality evidence is strong: Playwright critical-flow testing, Jest/Cypress skills, Storybook/UI standardization, and monorepo build pipeline improvements from 15 minutes to 3 minutes.
Senior influence and mentoring are supported by architecture decisions, process improvements, and mentoring five developers through pairing, code reviews, and workshops.

gaps.reason: Fault tolerance and security evidence is strong through OpenZeppelin Relayer, but explicit compliance evidence is not recorded. The resume should avoid claiming compliance ownership unless clarified.

Show client-funds protection through OpenZeppelin Relayer reliability, signer integrations, nonce safety, alert tuning, and secure audit workflows.
Surface mentoring, architecture influence, and process improvement after the technical/domain proof.
Keep API, WebSockets, frontend testing, and build tooling visible in skills and bullets where supported.
Use cryptocurrency and blockchain terms only around OpenZeppelin, Solidity, Relayer, EVM, RPC, and signer evidence.

Repeat in summary, skills, and frontend architecture bullets.

Translate to Playwright, Jest, Cypress, and React Testing Library where space allows.

Use around OpenZeppelin, EVM, Solidity, Relayer, RPC, and signer integrations.

evidence.bullet: Senior Full-Stack Engineer with 12+ years of professional experience building and operating production systems across B2B SaaS, enterprise platforms, marketplaces, and Web3 security.
Co-led Defender 2.0 Secure Audit as one of two engineers, building a full-stack module with a frontend focus in TypeScript and React.
Built a presentation feature in MyPremise with React and TypeScript that surfaced up-to-date, often live data.
Built React and TypeScript frontend modules and contributed to React/Next.js initiatives.
Built React Native mobile applications for client products; user clarified these included iOS/Android apps for Eucerin training, medical clinic training, and employee check-in/check-out use cases.
Led architecture decisions and set engineering standards for TypeScript, React, and React Native across the company.
Contributed to OIF and Ascend initiatives with React and Next.js, shaping frontend architecture decisions including SSR vs SPA, state management, and i18n.
Tech stack includes React, Next.js, TypeScript, Express.js, GraphQL, Apollo GraphQL, Material-UI, Tailwind CSS, Storybook, and Jest.
Contributed to the migration from REST to GraphQL with Apollo across key entities, including Express.js service updates, improving API response times by 35%.
Implemented transaction-related API flows with Express.js, including integrations with Transbank for payment processing use cases.
Used WebSockets for real-time delivery coordinate transmission so customers could see where their order was coming from.
Backend & APIs includes REST APIs and WebSockets.
Led end-to-end testing for Defender with Playwright, focusing on critical user flows to improve release confidence.
Improved release reliability by expanding test coverage with Jest and adding quality gates in GitHub Actions.
Testing & QA includes Playwright, React Testing Library, Jest, Cypress, and Pytest.

Refactored the TypeScript monorepo build system with ESM migration, package exports, bundler and tsconfig updates, plus lint and typecheck flow, reducing build times from about 15 minutes to 3 minutes.

Helped standardize the UI with Material UI and introduced Storybook for shared component documentation and design handoff, reducing prototyping time from one week to one day while improving consistency and reducing UI bugs.
Delivered more than five web and mobile products for clients across multiple sectors, working end to end from discovery and requirements to planning and implementation.
Worked remotely for OpenZeppelin on Defender, OSS Relayer, and React/Next.js initiatives across Web3 security and developer infrastructure.
Built merchant ingestion and onboarding systems for a fast-growing marketplace platform using Go, Node.js, Angular, Kafka, Redis, and Kubernetes.
English (Advanced - C1).
Supported Defender sunset by improving documentation and working directly with enterprise users during migration to OSS Relayer.
Built Stripe billing integration in Defender 2.0 for self-serve Pro subscriptions and enterprise-tier plans.
Implemented transaction-related API flows with Express.js, including integrations with Transbank for payment processing use cases.
Implemented payment gateway integrations with Express.js services, including Transbank transaction flows for client projects.

Owned the EVM side of the Rust-based OpenZeppelin Relayer OSS work, including chain-specific transaction cost handling, RPC balancing/retry/failover, cloud KMS signer integrations, and a fix for a critical nonce race condition.
Cut alert noise by about 60% across Slack and PagerDuty by tuning recoverable RPC and gas-spike alerts.
Built a React and TypeScript presentation feature that surfaced up-to-date, often live data through a single shareable URL, reducing friction for users sharing reports.
Co-led Defender 2.0 Secure Audit as one of two engineers, building tools for smart contract auditors to work with clients, track findings, verify fixes, and deliver final PDF reports.
Owned EVM Relayer work including AWS KMS and GCP KMS signer integrations and a fix for a critical nonce race condition.

Contributed to OIF and Ascend initiatives with React and Next.js, shaping frontend architecture decisions including SSR vs SPA, state management, i18n, and infrastructure direction with Kubernetes and Okta.
Worked with product, engineering, and infrastructure teams to simplify release flow and reduce delivery cycles from months to about two weeks.
Mentored five developers through pairing sessions, code reviews, and workshops focused on architecture, testing, and code quality.
Improved release reliability with Jest and GitHub Actions quality gates, reducing release cycles from one week to less than 24 hours by cutting manual testing effort.
Owned EVM Relayer reliability capabilities including RPC balancing/retry/failover, signer integrations, transaction cost handling, and a critical nonce race fix.

Engineered a Redis-based conflict resolution layer that prioritized real-time event processing over scheduled tasks, handling duplicate and out-of-order updates while improving product data accuracy and reliability by 40%.
Cut alert noise by about 60% across Slack and PagerDuty by tuning recoverable RPC and gas-spike alerts.
Worked on Web3 security products, smart contract auditor workflows, Defender SDK capabilities, OSS Relayer, Solidity-adjacent systems, and EVM transaction infrastructure.
Languages and OpenZeppelin tech stack include Rust, Solidity, TypeScript, React, AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform, RPC/Relayer-adjacent tooling, and Web3 developer infrastructure.
